ctxMarket: Attention is on Solana’s record-high user activity and stable application revenue, even as SOL’s price saw a notable Q1 decline. Security concerns are also in focus following reports of targeted malware campaigns against crypto developer environments.
ctxTalking:
• Researchers identified TrapDoor malware targeting Solana, Aptos, and Sui developer tools.
• Solana’s application revenue remained steady above $342 million despite market volatility.
• MoonPay’s integration allows crypto purchases, including SOL, directly within ChatGPT.
ctxMatters: Security threats like TrapDoor highlight ongoing risks for Solana developers and ecosystem projects. Sustained application revenue and user activity suggest continued engagement with Solana-based apps, regardless of price swings. New purchase channels via AI platforms could broaden access and visibility for Solana.
ctxSignal:
Signal: Record Solana activity and persistent developer-targeted malware campaigns.
Noise: Short-term price movements disconnected from underlying network usage.
ctxGlossary:
TrapDoor — Malware campaign targeting crypto developer environments via code repositories.
MoonPay — A payment platform enabling crypto purchases, now integrated with ChatGPT.

readMore.title: Researchers flag TrapDoor malware campaign targeting crypto developer environments including Aptos, Sui and Solana
Now You Can Buy Bitcoin, XRP and More in ChatGPT via MoonPay
